summary: Matched three previously stubbed Kirby special-move callbacks in ftKb_SpecialN.c: ftKb_SpecialHi1_Anim, ftKb_SpecialAirS_Enter, and ftKb_EatJump1_Anim. The PR reports 100% objdiff verification for all three functions and a clean ninja build.

key_files.change: Replaced three nonmatching function markers with matched C implementations for Kirby special callbacks.

what_changed: Implemented ftKb_SpecialHi1_Anim. When the animation has no frames remaining, it calls ftCommon_8007D5D4, changes Kirby to ftKb_MS_SpecialAirHi2 with flags 0xA, installs efLib_PauseAll and efLib_ResumeAll as hitlag callbacks, and clears fp->mv.kb.specialhi.x0.

Implemented ftKb_SpecialAirS_Enter. It initializes aerial Hammer state by clearing fp->cmd_vars[0], applying da->specials_aerial_vertical_momentum to fp->self_vel.y only if fp->fv.kb.x64 is not already set, setting fp->fv.kb.x64 true, changing to ftKb_MS_SpecialAirS, calling ftAnim_8006EBA4, and assigning accessory4_cb to fn_800F53AC.
Implemented ftKb_EatJump1_Anim. On animation end, it transitions to ftKb_MS_EatJump2 with flags 0x92, calls ftKb_SpecialN_800F9070, ftAnim_8006EBA4, and ftCo_800CB110 using da->specialn_jump_height.
smart_moves: Kept the matched implementations localized to the existing Kirby special-move source file rather than broadening the PR scope.

Used existing engine helpers and callbacks instead of introducing new abstractions: GET_FIGHTER, ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining, Fighter_ChangeMotionState, ftCommon_8007D5D4, ftAnim_8006EBA4, efLib_PauseAll, and efLib_ResumeAll.
Preserved one-shot behavior for Kirby aerial Hammer vertical momentum by gating the velocity write on fp->fv.kb.x64.
Used PAD_STACK in functions that required stack-shape padding for matching, with different padding amounts matching the local codegen needs.
decomp_lessons: For animation callbacks, the common match pattern is to guard transition logic with if (!ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining(gobj)) before changing motion state.

Kirby special-state transitions often combine Fighter_ChangeMotionState with follow-up helper calls and callback field updates; these side effects are part of the match and should not be omitted as gameplay-only details.
Per-character dat_attrs access through fp->dat_attrs is needed for tunable constants such as specials_aerial_vertical_momentum and specialn_jump_height.
Boolean or flag fields in fighter vars, such as fp->fv.kb.x64, may encode one-time-per-use behavior and can be critical to both logic and matching.
Stack padding macros like PAD_STACK(8) and PAD_STACK(24) remain useful matching tools when otherwise straightforward C logic has stack-layout differences.
naming_conventions: Kirby fighter functions use the ftKb_ prefix.
Callback names follow the motion/state plus callback phase pattern, such as ftKb_SpecialHi1_Anim, ftKb_SpecialAirS_Enter, and ftKb_EatJump1_Anim.
Kirby motion-state enum names use ftKb_MS_ prefixes, for example ftKb_MS_SpecialAirHi2, ftKb_MS_SpecialAirS, and ftKb_MS_EatJump2.
Kirby-specific per-fighter variables are accessed through fp->fv.kb, and Kirby-specific motion variables through fp->mv.kb.
Kirby data attributes are represented as ftKb_DatAttrs and accessed via fp->dat_attrs.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Inserted PAD_STACK(8) in ftKb_SpecialAirS_Enter and PAD_STACK(24) in ftKb_EatJump1_Anim to satisfy stack layout/codegen requirements.
Used exact motion-state flag constants from the matched output: 0xA for the SpecialHi1 to SpecialAirHi2 transition and 0x92 for the EatJump1 to EatJump2 transition.
Preserved exact floating-point arguments to Fighter_ChangeMotionState: start frame 0.0f, animation rate 1.0f, and blend/unknown argument 0.0f.
Matched callback assignment order in transition code, including hitlag callback setup after the Final Cutter motion-state change and accessory4_cb setup after ftAnim_8006EBA4 for aerial Hammer.
Verification evidence from the PR body says all functions matched 100% in objdiff and ninja built cleanly.

review_feedback.note: No review comments or formal reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body explicitly documented the three matched functions, their gameplay roles, and verification via objdiff plus ninja.

follow_up_queries: Find other Kirby special callbacks in ftKb_SpecialN.c still represented by /// # stubs.
Search for fp->fv.kb.x64 usage to understand the full lifecycle of Kirby aerial Hammer one-shot momentum.
Compare ftKb_SpecialS_Enter and ftKb_SpecialAirS_Enter for shared Hammer initialization patterns.
Search for other uses of ftCo_800CB110 with character-specific jump height dat_attrs.
Look for similar Final Cutter transition code around ftKb_SpecialHi2_Anim and ftKb_SpecialAirHi_Enter.
